title: reseda alba subsp. trigyna
intro: Reseda alba subsp. trigyna, also known as white smoke plant, is a plant species within the family Boraginaceae, characterized by its distinctive, fragrant, and pungent odour. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Reseda alba subsp. trigyna include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
